sections.paragraph:
δράξ, δρακός, ἡ (s. δράσσομαι; Batr. et al.; Aberciusins.; Herm. Wr. XXIII, 52, 486, 4 Sc.; LXX; ApcEsdr 7:5 p. 32, 17 [acc. δρακήν for δρακί Is 40:12]; Philo, Somn. 2, 74; Jos., Ant. 3, 251; 8, 322) the hand spread out, hand B 16:2 (Is 40:12).—DELG s.v. δράσσομαι.

Batr Batr , prob. not before I a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aberciusins. Aberciusins. = Aberciusinschrift—List 3

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

acc. acc. = accusative

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 261
